blocks.content: The entrance to Atogram was a 30-meter-high archway.
Despite its massive size, it didn't feel out of place because the city's walls were even taller.
User code confirmed. Entry permitted.
Unlike reality, everything here was automated, and the enormous iron gates opened to the sides.
Just the first sight made Shirone's head spin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow, there are so many!
Various advanced machines moved through the futuristic city.
The neon signs on the buildings flashed in so many colors that it was dizzying.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: We're meeting Yolga's Son in the evening, so let's hunt in the meantime. We need to level up.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: How about getting some parts first? Using just rifles is a bit annoying.
A team passing by saw Shirone's group and burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Other User
blocks.content: Ha! Look at those guys. Isn't that an old helmet? Do they still have those?

blocks.speaker: Other User
blocks.content: Reminds me of the old days. We used to catch Metal Rats with those. Are the Tenmen still around?
Destroyer707 stomped the ground.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Damn it! If I hadn't cleaned my codename, they wouldn't even talk to me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You said you were level 180, right? Maybe those users are higher. This place gathers high-level users.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: No, just by looking, they're barely level 100. You can tell by the parts. They're from Spread Corp, which only deals with mass-produced items. If they're over level 100 and still using those, it's a joke.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: When you reach Atogram, you usually gather about 20,000 galaxies. The standard is to buy a package of parts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What about selective options? It keeps blinking that I can buy something from the 'Electronic Store'.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Turn off the beginner tips. They're annoying while hunting. I'll explain. First, go into the selective options.
Following her instructions, a slot appeared with an 'Electronic Store' indicator below it.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: You can buy functions for the selective options from the Electronic Store. But right now, there's only 'Communication,' which costs 20,000 galaxies. It's less efficient than buying a package unless you're doing organized activities like the Tenmen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, we can communicate verbally. But there's only one slot. Do I add functions there?

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Yes. You'll get one more slot at level 20 and another at level 30, for a total of three options. From level 40, new functions will be registered in the 'Electronic Store' every 10 levels. Then you can choose options based on your preference, situation, or mission.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: If Yolga's Son is supporting you, you can buy communication, but it's a matter of preference. We have time until evening, so let's buy a package and hunt.
Destroyer707 looked at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: By the way, are you sure Yolga's Son will help you? Will you get galaxies too?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Probably. I don't know how much he can help, but he'll have to give everything he's got.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: It was a matter of humanity's future.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really? You must be really close.
There seemed to be a slight misunderstanding, but there was no need to explain in detail.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha, well…
Destroyer707 pointed to the city.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Let's go to 'Maron.' It's the best for beginners.
Entering the commercial district specializing in parts, they found it crowded with people.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There are so many.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: The most important thing in High Gear after levels is parts. There are 270 companies with shops here. Some are even run by users.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Users?

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Of course. Now users can exchange all items. Some sell parts at stalls, and some buy shops with galaxies and open stores.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Be careful with user-made parts. They often have unique or excellent functions but are weak in balance. My right arm is from Megamix, and if it's not compatible, it's a waste of money.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: That's your story.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: That's why I'm telling you! If we had plenty of galaxies, we could experiment with anything, but we're poor. We have to buy carefully.
When they arrived at 'Maron,' a humanoid machine with visible metal frames approached them.

blocks.speaker: Guide Robot
blocks.content: Welcome. How can I help you?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: We want to buy parts. The beginner Maron package. It's still 20,000 galaxies, right? When's the sale period?
The friendly guide robot brought a box larger than a person.
They put a lot of effort into the packaging.
The box featured a cute girl winking as if begging to be bought.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: All commercial parts come in boxes. Once opened, they're non-refundable. Some users collect them for specific models.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So we can't check the contents. Well, it's a program, so there shouldn't be defects.

blocks.speaker: Guide Robot
blocks.content: There are no defects. But you can check the contents. There's an AI simulation mark on the box. Touch it for a virtual experience.
Swallowing the words 'This is already virtual,' Shirone reached for the box.
An electric signal scanned Shirone, and he was suddenly equipped with the parts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?
The appearance was almost the same, but bearings were in every joint, and the seams were visible on the face.

blocks.speaker: Guide Robot
blocks.content: Hehe, interesting, right? You can experiment and buy if you like it. But as Death Princess said, user-sold items don't have this function, so be careful.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Joints…
Shirone rotated his elbow 360 degrees and turned his neck to see his back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's really strange.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: It'll feel unfamiliar at first. But ball joints are very convenient in combat. Of course, durability issues arise later. Anyway, let's buy. We can't afford anything else.
Shirone placed his palm on the 'Purchase' in the box and paid the galaxies, just like his companions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Choose immediate replacement. You'll experience it later anyway, but it's a bit strange.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Okay, immediate replacement.
After the transaction, the replacement process was skipped, and the four of them became androids.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's hunt now. You'll get used to the parts with a bit of play. So, where should we start?

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Since we don't have a nationality, we should go to the neutral zone. It'll be annoying there too, but…
Exiting Atogram's north gate, they saw an abandoned city 1 kilometer away.
Creepy creatures inhabited it, and some users were happily hunting.
Spatial Information: Destroy-033.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Destroy is a basic hunting ground in High Gear. Since we're level 10, let's target the war robots. See that one?
A machine with triangular legs stomping the ground and looking around caught their attention.
As the auto-aim locked on, Shirone's right arm underwent a mechanical transformation.
Ching! Ching!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whoa!

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: It looks weird, but bear with it. For beginner parts, this is pretty good. The main firepower of these parts is energy bullets. See the vertical gauge on the side of your view?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah, it says 1,000/1,000.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Energy bullets use a battery. One battery can fire 1,000 shots. Other companies sell parts that use real bullets, but galaxies can't cover that. It's less efficient too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How much is a battery?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 500 galaxies. That's affordable even if you mess up hunting. Let's start.
Gunfire echoed through Destroy-033.
The speed and firepower were incomparable to when they were in bio mode.
Bang! Bang!
Every time the four of them concentrated their energy bullets, the war robots shattered with a satisfying sound.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Five hundred shots left.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But we've already gathered 2,000 galaxies. A bit more, and we'll level up.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: …His adaptability is amazing. He said it was gross before.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 'He said it's his first time in High Gear, but this guy has fought a lot.'

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: He had watched rankers' raids via satellite broadcasts.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: All the strategies, tactics, reflexes, and judgment showed that skills were more than just functions.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 'His level is low, but…'

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Watching Yahweh2's combat, the thrill he felt from the rankers came rushing back like déjà vu.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 'Who is this guy?'
For the first time, he was curious about his identity, but Destroyer707 shook his head.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: He wouldn't like it if someone tried to uncover his real identity.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 'Let's just hunt.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Dodge!
Bang!
At the same time, sparks flew from Death Princess's body.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Ugh!
As the remaining three turned their heads, they saw someone smirking 250 meters away.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 'User codename…'

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Chaos5MinutesBefore.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: West Country.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Dodge!
Bang!
Death Princess's left arm was shattered.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: 'Penetration judgment.'
It was clear he had aimed deliberately, and Shirone turned around with a fierce look.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Haha! Sorry! Another mistake. I'll be really careful from now on, so please forgive me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's fight. He's a jerk.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: If we retaliate, it becomes hostile action. If we die in that state, we lose our loot. That's what he's aiming for…
Before he could finish, Shirone kicked off the ground and fired his energy gun at Chaos5MinutesBefore.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Haha! You shot? Now it's hostile action!
The level 23 AI was automatically dodging Shirone's bullets.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: 'Heh, Maron parts are easy.'

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: This is what a bolt gun is.
His energy bullets fired with a much heavier sound than Shirone's weapon.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?
He kept dodging the barrage of bullets and continued to counterattack.
The distance was closing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: W-What?

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Chaos5MinutesBefore's evasion probability was rapidly dropping to zero.
